description_full:
Given a string s containing an out-of-order English representation of digits 0-9, return the digits in ascending order.

 
Example 1:
Input: s = "owoztneoer"
Output: "012"
Example 2:
Input: s = "fviefuro"
Output: "45"

 
Constraints:


	1 <= s.length <= 105
	s[i] is one of the characters ["e","g","f","i","h","o","n","s","r","u","t","w","v","x","z"].
	s is guaranteed to be valid.
description: Given a string s containing an out-of-order English representation of digits 0-9, return the digits in ascending order.
constraints_text:
1 <= s.length <= 105
	s[i] is one of the characters ["e","g","f","i","h","o","n","s","r","u","t","w","v","x","z"].
	s is guaranteed to be valid.

subtasks.intent: Count character frequencies in the input string to prepare for digit deduction.
Calculate the count of each digit (0-9) using a greedy strategy based on unique character patterns.
Assemble the final result string by concatenating digits in ascending order based on their calculated counts.
